model.label: Kimi K2.5
model.description: Moonshot AI • Reasoning model from the Kimi family built for complex thinking tasks.

pick.rationale: Arkansas holds a commanding 80+ spot advantage in verified advanced metrics with a KenPom ranking of 17th and the nation's 5th-ranked offense, complemented by a solid 50th-ranked defense. While Hawai'i's elite defensive rating (8th nationally) and 2016 NCAA Tournament upset experience as a #13 seed create a credible upset path in this strength-versus-strength matchup, the Rainbow Warriors' weaker offensive profile (177th) and significantly lower strength of schedule face a severe mismatch against the SEC Tournament champions. Arkansas' power conference pedigree and balanced two-way efficiency provide a reliable path to survive the historically perilous 4/13 seed line.

reasoningStep.title: Arkansas vs Hawai'i - 4/13 Seed Line Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arkansas enters as a heavy favorite with top-20 advanced metrics and elite offensive efficiency, while Hawai'i's elite defense and 2016 upset history create a credible but unlikely upset path. The ~80-spot ranking gap and SEC vs Big West quality differential favor the Razorbacks despite Hawai'i's defensive prowess.
evidence: Arkansas: KenPom 17th, Torvik 20th, Adj O 5th, Adj D 50th, 25-8 record, SEC Tournament champions
Hawai'i: No KenPom/Torvik ratings available, Def Rtg 8th nationally, Off Rtg 177th, SRS 4.85 (105th), SOS -3.59 (227th), 24-8 record, Big West champions
Historical context: 13 seeds upset 4 seeds ~20% of the time; Hawai'i upset Cal as 13 seed in 2016
